# Document Summarizer — Usage Guide

*Mode: direct · 🤖 needs an LLM endpoint*

Summarises any PDF or text file into a concise Markdown summary using your configured LLM.

## When to use it

Use this for a quick readable summary of a document — a report, a long text file, a contract. For asking specific questions of tabular data, use CSV Data Analyzer instead.

## Inputs

- **Document (PDF or text file)** (required) — accepts: PDF (.pdf) or text (.txt, .md, .csv, .log, .json, .xml, .html) — one file.
  - The document to summarise.
  - ⚠️ Scanned (image-only) PDFs have no extractable text and will summarise poorly; use a text PDF.

## How to run

1. Pick the document.
2. Click Run.
3. Read or download the Markdown summary.

## Output

Output is written to the **`Data/outputs/`** folder, named:

```
Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-summary.md
```

Files produced:

- **`YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-summary.md`** — A Markdown summary capturing the document's main points.

## Tips

Very long documents may be condensed heavily — for a specific section, point the skill at just that text.

## Troubleshooting

| If… | Then… |
|------|-------|
| Run errors mentioning the endpoint / model. | This skill needs an LLM. Start Ollama or set an endpoint in Settings. |
| Summary is thin or generic. | The PDF may be a scan with no text layer. Use a text-based document. |
